rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed enantioselective migratory insertion of an alkene into a Co-aryl bond generating stereoisomeric cobaltacyclic intermediates studied by DFT

key_intermediates.name: Co(III)-aryl alkene complex Int1A (pro-R coordination, acenaphthylene bound)

electron_flow: Within an inner-sphere Co(III) coordination sphere bearing a pyridyl-imine and an oxazoline (iPr) ligand, the coordinated acenaphthylene undergoes concerted migratory insertion into the Co-aryl bond, producing diastereomeric cobaltacyclic alkyl intermediates Int2A/Int2B via TS(1-2)A/B.
rate_determining_step: Migratory insertion of the alkene into the Co-aryl bond (TS(1-2))
notes: Computational protocol: Gaussian 16, TPSS-D3(BJ)/def2-SVP geometry optimizations in gas phase; PW6B95-D4/def2-TZVPP single points with SMD implicit solvation (acetone ε=20.493 and ethanol ε=24.852, modeling 1:1 acetone/EtOH mixture). Two diastereomeric insertion pathways (A vs B) are compared to rationalize stereoisomer generation; the favored pathway determines product enantio/diastereoselectivity but relative energies are not given on this page.
